patient.chief_complaint: isolated thrombocytopenia discovered incidentally or during workup for mucocutaneous bleeding (platelets typically <30 x10^9/L when symptomatic)
symptoms: isolated thrombocytopenia discovered incidentally or during workup for mucocutaneous bleeding (platelets typically <30 x10^9/L when symptomatic)
easy bruising (ecchymoses) disproportionate to minor trauma
epistaxis, gingival bleeding, or prolonged bleeding from minor cuts
menorrhagia in premenopausal women (may be presenting or dominant symptom)
preceded by viral illness 1-3 weeks prior in pediatric acute ITP (50-80% of children)
signs: petechiae — pinpoint non-blanching hemorrhages on skin and mucous membranes, especially lower extremities and pressure areas
no splenomegaly, lymphadenopathy, or systemic symptoms (absence of these is clinically important)
